_header.purpose: Web application error analysis dashboard with error rate trends and status code breakdowns
_header.scope: HTTP error metrics including error rate percentage, error count trends, and per-status-code analysis
_header.overview: Extracts the Errors row from the golden signals overview into a dedicated detail dashboard. Displays error rate as a percentage over time, a stat panel for instant error rate, and a stacked bar chart of errors by HTTP status code. Enables focused investigation of error patterns with drill-down from the Web Summary dashboard.
_header.dependencies: Mimir datasource (uid: mimir), backend http_errors_total and http_requests_total counter metrics
_header.interfaces: PromQL rate queries against http_errors_total and http_requests_total counters
_header.related: web-summary.json for overview, web-red.json for per-endpoint error breakdown
_header.implementation: Grafana dashboard JSON schema version 39 with time series, stat, and bar chart panels

title: Errors
description: Are users getting errors? The trend line shows error rate over time — look for the moment errors started climbing. The status code breakdown on the right tells you what kind of errors: 4xx means bad requests (client-side or missing resources), 5xx means the backend is failing. During an incident, use this dashboard to confirm the error is real and identify the failure mode, then go to RED for per-endpoint detail or Traces to find the failing request.

panels.title: Error Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of all HTTP requests returning 400+ status codes over time. A flat line near zero is normal. A step change upward usually means a deployment introduced a bug. A gradual climb may mean a dependency is degrading. Correlate the inflection point with deployments or upstream changes.

panels.title: Error Rate
panels.description: Current error rate as a single number. Green (<1%) is normal — some 404s and bad requests are expected. Yellow (1-5%) needs investigation. Red (>5%) means a significant fraction of users are getting failures.

panels.title: Errors by Status Code
panels.description: Error requests per second broken down by HTTP status code. This tells you the failure mode. 500 = internal server error (check logs). 502/503 = backend or load balancer issue. 404 = missing resources (usually harmless). 429 = rate limiting. A dominant status code points you to the root cause.
